notes: Eighteenth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Official materials say the school was founded in 2007.
Its vision says students will identify a need and actively take part in their democracy to meet that need.
The school says it is committed to developing the whole child through culturally responsive teaching that cultivates confidence and positive self-identity.
KIPP's statewide network says its mission is to create joyful, academically excellent schools that prepare students with the skills and confidence to pursue the paths they choose.
KIPP's statewide board pages list Rich Richards as board chair.
Official school pages list Dr. Stacy Shepard as principal.
Official materials say the school was founded in 2015.
The school says education is the most powerful weapon to create social change and that students deserve relevant, rigorous curricula.
A school improvement plan says all students receive a profound, life-changing education used to create social change.
Official pages say there are no shortcuts to academic success and emphasize more time in school learning alongside a strong culture of achievement and support.
KIPP North Carolina board pages list Rich Richards as board chair.
Official school pages list David Tell as principal.
Official materials trace the school's roots to 2001, when the original KIPP Gaston College Prep opened as one of the earliest KIPP schools in the country.
The school says the strength of the individual and the strength of the community are tied together in what Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. called "the fabric of inescapable mutuality."
Official materials say the school believes in bravery, urgency, self-awareness, and passion as part of its culture.
Its upper-school page says students are prepared to use their knowledge, skills, and character to strengthen their communities and fight against injustices.
KIPP's statewide board pages list Rich Richards as board chair.
Official school pages list Shawna Mayo and Natassha Jenkins as the campus principals.
Its mission says the school provides a hands-on learning experience guided by a cohesive curriculum integrating nature and supported by an active community of parents and teachers.
Official materials say the school began as a grassroots initiative in 2012 and became a public charter school in fall 2014.
The school says it follows the Basic School model inspired by Ernest L. Boyer.
Official pages describe the school as the only nature-based, holistic, tuition-free public charter school in the Charlotte region.
Its board page lists co-founder Abigail Jennings as chair of the Board of Trustees.
Official staff pages list Dr. Rebecca Friend as executive director.

schools.officialName: Union Prep Academy at Indian Trail

clues.text: Its mission says the school offers a learning environment that is academically rigorous and enhanced with personalized learning plans.
Official materials say the school is part of the Charter Schools USA family.
The school's public identity includes the rallying cry "Go Polar Bears!"
Official pages highlight dual enrollment, AP programming, and a dynamic academic community as part of the upper school experience.
Board pages say Union County Charter Education Association is the founding board for the school.
Official administration pages list Jesse Hogan as interim principal for the lower school and Mr. Lis as principal for the upper school.
